MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-02-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE IS DENIED. THE PROCESS SERVER'S DECLARATION ESTABLISHES THAT FOUR ATTEMPTS WERE MADE BY PLAINTIFF TO PERSONALLY SERVE DEFENDANT AT HIS RESIDENTIAL ADDRESS BEFORE SUBSTITUTED SERVICE WAS MADE. PLAINTIFF CARRIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT REASONABLE ATTEMPTS WERE MADE TO PERSONALLY SERVE DEFENDANT BEFORE RESORTING TO SUBSTITUTED SERVICE UNDER CCP 415.20(B). DEFENDANT'S DECLARATION DOES NOT SET FORTH SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT ESTABLISHES CONTRARY FACTS. PARTIES STIPULATE THAT MR. ENG HAS 60 DAYS FROM ENTRY OF ORDER TO FILE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Notice Of Motion To Quash Service
Off Calendar Jul-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ADRIENNE HADDAD, ESQ., 510.469.6011, FOR PLAINTIFF ALLIANCE CREDIT SERVICES, INC.
MARTIN ENG, DEFENDANT IN PRO PER.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG's Motion To Quash Service is denied. The process server's declaration establishes that four attempts were made by Plaintiff to personally serve Defendant at his residential address before substituted service was made. Plaintiff carried its burden of showing that reasonable attempts were made to personally serve Defendant before resorting to substituted service under CCP 415.20(b). Defendant's declaration does not set forth sufficient evidence that establishes contrary facts. PARTIES STIPULATE THAT MR. ENG HAS 60 DAYS FROM ENTRY OF ORDER TO FILE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Of Motion To Quash Service
Off Calendar Jul-02-2013 Continued To Jul-26-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG's Motion To Quash Service is continued to July 26, 2013 to give Plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 2, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and papers. Judge: Richard Kramer, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/RAK)
